compiled_state.title: As a project maintainer, I can provision a GitHub Ruleset that blocks direct pushes and requires CI status checks
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: A Bash script that creates a GitHub Ruleset via gh api POST, blocking direct pushes to main and requiring specified CI status checks to pass before merge.
**Why**: The Ruleset is the enforcement mechanism — without it, CI checks are informational only.
**Scope**:
- IN: Ruleset creation script, default branch detection, pre-flight checks (gh version, admin scope), manual guide fallback, auto-merge enablement, admin bypass actor (RepositoryRole actor_id 5)
- OUT: CI workflow creation (Story 1), LLM review integration (Story 3), onboarding orchestration (Story 4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, running the provisioning script creates a Ruleset on the target repository that blocks direct pushes to main and requires all specified CI status checks to pass before merge
  ← Satisfies: "After onboarding completes, direct pushes to main are blocked"
- When this story is complete, the script detects the default branch dynamically and includes an admin bypass actor for outage scenarios
  ← Satisfies: "LLM review job includes admin Ruleset bypass actor so PRs can still merge during provider outages"
- When this story is complete, pre-flight checks validate gh CLI version and admin permissions, falling back to a manual setup guide if either check fails
  ← Satisfies: "onboarding with pre-flight checks for gh version and admin permissions — emitting a manual setup guide"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Integration] The Ruleset script must know the exact job/check names from the CI workflow (Story 1) to configure required_status_checks. These names must be coordinated — either accepted as parameters, read from config, or discovered from the workflow file.
- [Security] The script requires admin permissions. Never log or echo credential values. Pre-flight failure must produce a clear, actionable error.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent. "High confidence" means clear evidence from the codebase or ticket context — not inference or reasonable assumption. When in doubt, stop and ask rather than guess.

comments.body: Done definition update (2026-04-26 scope expansion, epic 3aaa-0238 SC8/SC9/SC10): Required-check list expands from 4 (lint, format, tests, LLM review) to 11 by adding: validate-ci (from d025-87d4), three platform-matrix legs linux-bash4/macos-bash3/alpine-busybox (from new story 792b-eb80), and pipefail-strict (from new story 4383-9234). Coordinate check-context naming with each upstream story.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Done definition update (adversarial review): Define a single source-of-truth artifact (e.g., `.github/required-checks.txt` or a YAML map under .github/) that lists the canonical check-context names. The Ruleset provisioner reads this file when constructing the API payload, and a CI lint step asserts every name in the file matches an actual workflow job/step that emits that context. This prevents silent name drift across d025-87d4, 792b-eb80, 4383-9234, and fc8b-aab0. Without it, GitHub matrix jobs (e.g., 'test (linux, bash4)') will not match the bare names assumed in the epic description, causing phantom required checks.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Consideration (adversarial review): [Conflicting Assumptions] The bypass-actor RepositoryRole actor_id 5 is the built-in Admin role on standard GitHub repos, but org-owned repos with custom role schemes may shift these IDs. The provisioner (and onboarding in a592-0992) must include a pre-flight that: (a) verifies the calling gh token has admin scope on the target repo; (b) verifies actor_id 5 resolves to Admin (gh api /repos/{owner}/{repo}/collaborators/permission or org role API); emit a manual-setup guide and continue without the Ruleset if either check fails.
comments.author: Test
